{"success": true, "name": "client-content-audit", "description": "Audit a client repo's content against a questionnaire/source-of-truth — find gaps, fix them, and surface what the client still needs to provide. Also covers pure business/client intelligence extraction from existing repo docs (team, business model, competitive position, market segments, founder profile, pricing, brand) without content implementation — for when the user wants business/strategic analysis, not technical changes to the site. Covers questionnaire ingestion, gap analysis, source-of-truth generation, content implementation, and in-repo business intelligence synthesis.", "tags": ["client", "content", "audit", "questionnaire", "gap-analysis", "paraguai", "dayah", "el-viajero", "business-intelligence", "strategic-analysis"], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: client-content-audit
description: "Audit a client repo's content against a questionnaire/source-of-truth — find gaps, fix them, and surface what the client still needs to provide. Also covers pure business/client intelligence extraction from existing repo docs (team, business model, competitive position, market segments, founder profile, pricing, brand) without content implementation — for when the user wants business/strategic analysis, not technical changes to the site. Covers questionnaire ingestion, gap analysis, source-of-truth generation, content implementation, and in-repo business intelligence synthesis."
tags: [client, content, audit, questionnaire, gap-analysis, paraguai, dayah, el-viajero, business-intelligence, strategic-analysis]
---

# Client Content Audit & Questionnaire Implementation

Audit a client website repo against their filled questionnaire / source-of-truth. Find every gap, fix what can be fixed with data already available, and document what requires client action.

## When to Use

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated
- **Pre-build questionnaire design** — client hasn't filled anything yet, you need to produce a structured intake form for a NEW client type not in the builder. Use the Block-style MC format (see references/questionnaire-ABCDE-pattern.md).
- **Business intelligence extraction** — user wants pure strategic/business analysis (team structure, business model, revenue model, competitive position, market segments, founder story, brand identity, pricing model, gaps/risks) WITHOUT any content implementation or website changes. Do NOT touch content files or code — synthesize what's already in the repo docs.

## Business Intelligence Extraction Mode

When the user says "foco en el negocio y cliente, no en la parte web" or explicitly asks for strategic/business analysis of a client without website changes:

### What to Extract

Read ALL of these files from the repo in one pass:

| Priority | File | What It Tells You |
|----------|------|-------------------|
| 1 | `docs/01-client/client-intelligence.json` | Team roles, vertical, status, owner, account manager, locale info |
| 2 | `docs/01-client/client-questionnaire-responses.md` | Services breakdown, process steps, founder story, pricing model |
| 3 | `docs/01-client/client-update-questionnaire.md` | What's pending client confirmation, what was rejected, pending gaps |
| 4 | `docs/client-analysis-complete.md` | Prior analysis — executive summary, what they want, what was rejected, business model, competitive position, immediate priorities |
| 5 | `docs/client-feedback-*.md` | Latest client feedback — nav changes, content removals, feature rejections, approvals |
| 6 | `docs/03-brand/brand-guide.md` | Brand name, taglines, color palette, typography, logo status, tone of voice by locale, social links |
| 7 | `docs/09-market-intelligence/customer-segments/customer_persona_dossiers.md` | Deep persona research — driving factors, income range, decision triggers, best channels, marketing messages, segment size |
| 8 | Site config (`site.json`, `content/*.json`) | Pricing, features, contact details, locale setup |
| 9 | `CLAUDE.md` or `README.md` | Deployment info, tech stack, live URL, WhatsApp bot config |

### Output Structure

Synthesize into a clear picture covering:

```
**CLIENT (founder name)**
- Background, personal story, why they started this
- Launch stage (pre-launch / active / scaling)
- Ethical stance / dealbreakers (e.g. "no fabricated data")

**WHAT THEY WANT**
- Site vision in their own words
- Core features/services (list them)
- Process (if applicable)
- What content should be unique to dedicated pages vs summarized on homepage
- Blog/content strategy

**WHAT THEY REJECTED**
- Specific features they said no to
- Why (e.g. "fabricated stats", "no real clients yet")

**TEAM**
- Account manager / client liaison
- Client success / operations
- Who owns the project

**BUSINESS MODEL**
- Services offered (grouped)
- Pricing model (public vs private, packages vs a la carte)
- Target customer segments (by country, income, motivation)
- Revenue stage / status
- Distinct differentiators (what makes them unique)

**MARKET LANDSCAPE**
- Key customer segments (by source country)
- Push factors (tax policy changes, legislation)
- Competitive differentiators vs alternatives (Portugal, Thailand, etc.)
- Total addressable demand and growth trend

**KEY RISKS & GAPS**
- What's missing (real clients, data, photos, integrations)
- What needs client action vs what can be done now

**TONE**
- Always in the user's language (mix of Spanish/English)
- Bullet points, no markdown on WhatsApp
- Prioritize business insights over technical architecture
```

### Key Behaviors

- **Do NOT** open content JSON files, page configs, or component files unless the user explicitly asks for website changes
- **Do NOT** propose technical fixes (deployment, Docker, builds) unless asked
- **Do NOT** write any code or modify any files
- **Prioritize** reading from `docs/01-client/`, `docs/03-brand/`, `docs/09-market-intelligence/` directories first
- **Synthesize across files** — client intelligence, feedback docs, and analysis docs often tell different parts of the same story. Cross-reference them for a complete picture.
- **Surface contradictions** — if feedback says "remove testimonials" but analysis says "testimonials exist", flag it
- **Context matters** — who is Sonia? Who is Kyrian? What language does the client speak? All this lives in client-intelligence.json
- **Save a compact memory entry** after extraction so future sessions know the client's key facts without re-reading everything

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated
- **Pre-build questionnaire design** — client hasn't filled anything yet, you need to produce a structured intake form for a NEW client type not in the builder. Use the Block-style MC format (see references/questionnaire-ABCDE-pattern.md).

## Process

### 1. Ingest the Questionnaire

The questionnaire may be in `docs/` as `questionnaire.md`, `source-of-truth.md`, or `client-questionnaire.md`. Three tiers of detail:

- **`client-questionnaire.md`** — blank template (2KB, section headings only, no answers)
- **`questionnaire.md`** — filled form with client's answers (15-20KB)
- **`source-of-truth.md`** — structured canonical reference (10-15KB)

Read all three. The `source-of-truth.md` is the most authoritative — if it exists, use it as primary.

### 2. Map Questionnaire -> Content Inventory

Check every section of the questionnaire against the app's content:

```python
import json

# Read content
content = json.load(open("content/es.json"))
content_str = json.dumps(content).lower()

# Check each field
gaps = []
if "5618487" not in content_str:
    gaps.append("RUC not in content")
```

### 3. Categorize Gaps

Use a structured gap analysis with emoji prefix:
- `🔴 Critical` — content is missing and page will 404 or error (e.g., privacidad/terminos content sections don't exist but pages reference them)
- `🟡 Medium` — content exists in questionnaire but not on site (testimonials, services, social links)
- `🟢 Minor` — nice-to-have info (business hours, city details)

### 4. Fix What Can Be Fixed NOW

**Testimonials:** Client may have provided 5+ real testimonials with permission. Replace the placeholder/generic ones. Include: name, country, service received, quote, rating. DO NOT fabricate names — use verified client names only.

**Social Links:** The client's questionnaire almost always has Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Wattpad, Amazon Author links that are nowhere on the site. Add them to the footer's `social` array and to the contact section.

**Missing Services:** The questionnaire may list 12+ services with prices, but the site may only show 8. Add the missing ones. Each needs: id, name, description, priceUSD, pricePYG, delivery time.

**Education/Credentials:** Client certifications (MIT, Google, etc.) and education rarely make it onto the about page. Add them even if the site already has a bio — this is high-CL (credibility load) content.

**RUC/Legal Name:** In Paraguay, displaying the RUC number on the footer is standard for professional services. Add it if missing.

**Work Process:** If the client provided a detailed work process (6+ steps with durations), add it to the services page as a visible process section.

**Blog:** If content has blog posts (`blog.posts`) but no `/blog` route exists, create the pages. Posts often have slug, title, excerpt, date, category, author, readingTime.

### 5. Sync to English (or secondary locale)

Most LATAM client sites are bilingual (ES/EN). After fixing ES content, sync the same changes to EN. Watch out for different key structures — ES may use `about.story.paragraphs` while EN uses `sobre.bio.features`. Check keys before blind-copying.

### 6. Generate/Save source-of-truth.md

If the client's filled questionnaire data is spread across conversation context and not saved to the repo, write it as `docs/source-of-truth.md`. This file becomes THE canonical reference for all future work. Structure it as:

```markdown
# Client Name — Source of Truth

> Complete client questionnaire answers. Canonical reference for all content.
> Source: Client onboarding session — Month Year

## PART A — CONFIRMATIONS

### A.1 Brand Identity
| Field | Confirmed Value | Implemented |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | ✅ | ✅ |
| RUC | [REDACTED_PHONE] | ❌ NOT IN CONTENT |

### A.3 Services with Prices ✅
| # | Service | USD | PYG | Delivery | Implemented |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Custom Cover eBook | $45 | ₲300,000 | 1-2 weeks | ✅ |
```

### 7. Client Drive Asset Ingestion

When the client shares a Google Drive folder with images/assets, DO NOT treat as "client action needed" — ingest them now. This is faster than waiting.

#### 7a. Download the Folder

```bash
# Install gdown if not present
pip install gdown

# Download entire folder by link
python3 -m gdown --folder "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/<FOLDER_ID>" -O /tmp/<project-assets>

# Or for a single file by share link
python3 -m gdown "https://drive.google.com/uc?id=<FILE_ID>" -O /tmp/<output.ext>
```

#### 7b. Analyze Without Vision

When vision APIs are down, classify images by **metadata + naming patterns**:

```python
from PIL import Image
import os

path = '/tmp/assets'
files = sorted([f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith(('.jpg','.png','.jfif','.webp'))])

for f in files:
    size_kb = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024
    img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
    w, h = img.size
    ratio = w/h
    
    # Classify by aspect ratio
    if ratio > 1.4:    shape = "WIDE — banner/badge row"
    elif ratio > 1.0:  shape = "LANDSCAPE — card/certificate"
    elif ratio > 0.85: shape = "SQUARISH — profile/badge"
    elif ratio > 0.7:  shape = "PORTRAIT — ranking/vertical"
    else:              shape = "TALL — individual item"
    
    # Classify by dimensions
    if w == 1080 and h == 2400:
        cat = "PHONE SCREENSHOT (Amazon KDP/Instagram analytics)"
    elif w == 1080 and h == 1350:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM POST"
    elif '_n.jpg' in f:
        cat = "INSTAGRAM-SOURCED"
    elif '-' in f and len(f.split('-')[0]) == 8:
        cat = f"UUID CROP ({shape})"
    else:
        cat = f"UNKNOWN ({shape})"
    
    print(f"  {f:<55} {size_kb:>4}KB  {cat}")
```

**Key heuristics for Drive folder names that reveal content type:**
- `Logros` = Achievement screenshots (KDP rankings, Prime badges, reader counts, certifications)
- `Logo editoriales` = Publisher/editorial logos for client's partner section
- `Fotos`, `Imágenes`, `Assets` = General brand imagery
- Naming with underscore + number (`_n.jpg`) = Instagram downloads
- UUID filenames = Exported/cropped from an app or screenshot tool

#### 7c. Convert Problematic Formats

```python
# .jfif files won't render in browsers — convert to .png
from PIL import Image
for f in os.listdir(path):
    if f.endswith('.jfif'):
        img = Image.open(f'{path}/{f}')
        out = f.replace('.jfif', '.png')
        img.save(f'{path}/{out}', 'PNG')
        # Keep original too in case client wants it
```

#### 7d. Organize Into Repo

Place files in `public/<project>/<category>/`:
- `public/dayah/logos/` — publisher logos, brand marks
- `public/dayah/achievements/` — badges, rankings, certificates (new section)
- `public/dayah/social/` — Instagram/marketing posts (existing)

Update site components to reference the new asset paths (inline image map or content file).

### 8. What to Leave for Client Action

Some things cannot be fixed by the agent and must be surfaced as "Client Action Still Needed":
- **Missing real photos** (premade catalog, professional photography) — client still owes these
- **Final brand palette** (exact hex codes, typography) — pending client design decisions
- **Mercado Pago/PayPal confirmation** — client must verify account
- **Domain registration** — client must purchase and point DNS
- **Drive folders without clear naming** — ask client what the files are

## Reference Implementation

**Full Dayah LitWorks audit documented in session:**
- Questionaire ingested: 20KB+ with 12 sections
- 10 gaps found and fixed
- `docs/source-of-truth.md` generated (14KB)
- `docs/client-profile.md` — client achievement audit
- All changes synced ES/EN and pushed

**Google Drive asset ingestion (May 2026):**
- `references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md` — full inventory of 40 asset files from Drive folder "Logros", including dimension-based classification methodology used when vision API was unavailable
- Logo editoriales folder: 4 publisher logos downloaded, converted (.jfif→.png), committed to `public/dayah/logos/`, integrated into homepage and clientes page

## Corrections Document Implementation (Post-Audit)

After the initial audit, clients often send a corrections/updates document (structured MD file) with immediate action items. This is distinct from the initial gap analysis — it's an authoritative list of changes from the client themselves.

### When to Expect This

- Client reviews the live/preview site and sends a document titled "Correcciones", "Cambios", "Final edits", or similar
- Usually a .md file with checkboxes, ✅ / ❌ / ⬜ markers
- Often arrives close to launch date (1-2 weeks before)
- Client may have appended it to the conversation as a text document

### Signal Detection

If a user sends a structured document with sections like "IMPLEMENTAR YA", "ACTUALIZACIONES INMEDIATAS", or a checklist, it's a corrections doc. Do NOT treat it as conversation context alone — save it to document cache and work from it systematically.

### Implementation Process

#### 1. Parse the document structure

Corrections docs typically have these section types:
- **✅ Inmediatas** — things to remove/change NOW (book removals, testimonial replacements, payment method changes)
- **⚠️ Pending client** — marked with ⬜, client will send later (photos, bios, portfolio images)
- **❌ Rejections** — features client doesn't want (music, credits, payment integrations)
- **✅ Approvals** — things client CONFIRMS (color palette, logos, fonts, domain)

#### 2. Batch operations, one-by-one changes

```python
# Batch all JSON edits in one script execution
import json

with open('content/es.json') as f:
    es = json.load(f)

# Remove items by title
es['home']['books']['items'] = [b for b in es['home']['books']['items'] if b['title'] != 'La Efímera Eternidad del Amor']

# Replace arrays entirely
es['home']['testimonials'] = [new_testimonial_objects]

# Update URLs in bulk
book_links = {'Intense': 'https://rxe.me/7V2YVQ', ...}
for book in es['home']['books']['items']:
    if book['title'] in book_links:
        book['platformUrl'] = book_links[book['title']]

# Remove payment provider strings across all sections (FAQ, terms, privacy, footer)
for section in es['terminos']['sections']:
    if 'Mercado Pago' in section['a']:
        section['a'] = section['a'].replace('Mercado Pago, ', '')

with open('content/es.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(es, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
```

#### 3. Update CSS/design tokens from corrections

Corrections often include an approved color palette:
- Update `app/globals.css` `@theme` block
- Update `meta[name="theme-color"]` in layout.tsx
- Update `content/tokens.json` if used

#### 4. Fix pre-existing build errors discovered during rebuild

Corrections session inevitably involves `npm run build` to verify. Pre-existing errors (not caused by your changes) WILL surface. Fix them rather than treating them as blockers:

- **TypeScript index signature errors**: `content.blogPost?.[slug as string]` fails because JSON-inferred types lack index signatures. Fix: `(content.blogPost as Record<string, any>)?.[slug as string]`
- **Missing required props**: Components that changed signature after you started may lack props. Listen to the build error — it tells you the exact missing prop and component.
- **WhatsAppFloat phone prop**: This component requires `phone` prop. If any page was missing it, the build will fail on that specific page.

#### 5. Sync changes to secondary locale

Most changes (payment removal, palette, testimonials) apply to EN locale too. But only fix the keys that exist — don't add sections that don't exist in the secondary locale.

#### 6. When to save a reference file

If the corrections document is long (100+ lines) and contains detailed client instructions that future sessions may need (like exact book links, testimonial full text, color hexes), save it as a reference file under the skill:

```bash
skill_manage(action='write_file', name='client-content-audit', 
  file_path='references/dayah-corrections-[REDACTED_PHONE].md',
  file_content='<document content>')
```

### Founder Direct-Input Pattern (Chat-Based Second Source of Truth)

When the founder provides content directly through chat (WhatsApp message, voice memo transcription, typed message), this is often **more authoritative** than previously-documented data:

| Scenario | Old Data | New Data (Chat) | Action |
|----------|----------|-----------------|--------|
| Years abroad | "10+ years" | "7 years in the Netherlands" | UPDATE |
| Clients helped | "500+ families" (template default) | "10 people — all Dutch" | REPLACE (founder knows best) |
| Number of complex cases | Not documented | "none so far" | ADD |
| Best achievement | Not documented | "propiedades a buen precio evitando que les jodan" | ADD (this is the founder's pride) |
| What clients value most | Generic claim | "acompañamiento casi familiar" | USE THIS EXACT PHRASE |

**Process:**
1. Extract EVERY standalone fact from the chat and compare to existing content
2. Prioritize latest data — the founder's direct word > previously-documented data > template defaults > AI-generated values
3. Update `docs/client-analysis-complete.md` immediately (the analysis doc must reflect current facts)
4. Save the raw chat to `docs/client-feedback-<date>.md` for traceability
5. Embed the founder's exact phrases (they're authentic copy)

**⚠️ Zero fabricated data:** When the pre-built template has default stats (e.g. "500+ families"), and the founder says "10 clients, all Dutch", the template stats must be REMOVED entirely, not massaged to match. Having ZERO testimonials is better than having fake ones — the founder's ethics dictate the site's honesty standard.

Some client data points evolve over time. The founder may say "0 clients" in week 1 and "10 clients" in week 3. Track these as **living stats** — they get updated, not created:

| Stat | Initial Value | Updated Value | Date | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Clients helped | 0 | 10 (all Dutch) | May 9, 2026 | Sonia (founder-direct) |
| Complex cases | N/A | "none so far" | May 9, 2026 | Sonia |
| Client satisfaction | N/A | "acompañamiento casi familiar" | May 9, 2026 | Sonia |
| Key achievement | N/A | "propiedades a buen precio evitando que les jodan" | May 9, 2026 | Sonia |

**When to update the client analysis doc:** Any time the founder provides a new data point that changes the business narrative. Update `docs/client-analysis-complete.md` to reflect the new reality.

## Raw Requirements List Ingestion (Client-Provided Checklists)

When a client provides a structured list (e.g., "these are the requirements for residency, I want this on the page in every language"):

### Process
1. **Save the raw list** to a reference file first — this is the source of truth
2. **Check each locale** for existing requirements sections:
   - `aboutPage.requirements.basicDocuments.items` (NL/DE/EN structure)
   - Some locales may have it (`aboutPage.requirements` exists in NL and EN) while others don't (ES may be missing it)
3. **Structure the requirements data** into consistent subsections:
   - `basicDocuments` — flat list of documents
   - `importantNotes` — apostille/translation rules
   - `processedLocally` — what's handled in Paraguay
   - `residenceTypes` — temporary vs permanent
   - `mercosur` — simplified rules for Mercosur citizens
   - `costs` — approximate cost note
4. **Write ALL locales** in a single batch — don't do one at a time
5. **If a locale is missing the entire section structure**, create it at the right nesting level:
   - NL/EN: `aboutPage.requirements.{subsections}`
   - ES: Check if exists at `aboutPage.requirements` first
   - DE: Same structure as NL/EN
6. **Verify** by navigating to the about page in each locale and checking the requirements render

### Common Correction Patterns
|---|---|
| "Remove X from catalog" | Filter items array in JSON, check ALL locales |
| "Use ONLY these testimonials" | Replace entire testimonials array |
| "Eliminar música" | Remove audio elements, delete audio-related imports |
| "No credit to X" | Remove name/credits from about page, docs |
| "Paleta aprobada" | Update CSS theme block and meta theme-color |
| "Payment method X not available" | Remove from FAQ, terms, privacy, footer (all locales) |
| "Photo provisional" | Use existing photo, note it's temporary |
| "About section pending" | Keep current text, don't publish (if possible, add progress note) |

### What NOT to do

- Don't ask "which one" — the document is client-authoritative, just implement
- Don't re-audit — the client has already reviewed the site, trust their list
- Don't create new features unless the doc explicitly asks for them
- Don't renegotiate payment methods — if client says "MP not available in Paraguay", update immediately
- Don't ask for testimonials again — client already provided the authorized set
- Don't spend time on ⬜ items — those are client-pending, surface them in summary

## Multi-Locale Content Implementation (PAB Relocation Site Pattern)

When a client sends free-form text (WhatsApp message, questionnaire) with structured content that needs to go into a multi-locale JSON-driven site like Nexa Paraguay (en/es/nl/de, 4 locales, static Next.js + PAB):

### 1. Save Questionnaire Response First

Write the raw client response to `docs/client-questionnaire-responses.md` before touching any code. This is the source of truth.

### 2. Gap Analysis Structure

Produce a structured matrix before implementing:
| Area | Current Site | Client Wants | Action |
|---|---|---|---|

### 3. Batch JSON Manipulation (Critical)

**Do NOT use patch tool for large JSON content sections.** Patch is fragile with JSON — it can orphan sibling keys or leave stale content when fuzzy-matching multi-line blocks.

Instead:
- **Prefer direct JSON manipulation via python** — read with `json.load()`, modify dicts/lists, write with `json.dump(..., indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)`
- If you must use patch, target SMALL blocks (single key-value pair) with a unique anchor line
- Always validate after patch: `python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open(path))'`

### 4. Always Update ALL Locales

For LATAM relocation sites (en/es/nl/de), every content change must be replicated to all 4 locale files in the same session. The user will NOT ask you to do this — it's assumed.

Use a single Python script to batch-process all locale files:
```python
LANGUAGES = {"es": {...translations...}, "nl": {...}, "de": {...}, "en": {...}}
for lang, translations in LANGUAGES.items():
    data = json.load(open(f"content/{lang}.json"))
    data["home"]["services"] = translations["services"]
    ...
    json.dump(data, open(f"content/{lang}.json", "w"), indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
```

### 5. Update Page Configs Too

Content JSON changes (adding a new section like `home.services`) require corresponding page config updates:
- `pages/home.json` — add the section in the `sections` array
- `pages/programas.json` — if services should appear on programs page too
- `pages/sobre.json` — if adding story/narrative section

### 6. Verify and Commit

```bash
# Validate all files
for f in pages/*.json content/*.json; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))" && echo "$f: VALID"; done

git add -A && git commit -m "content: ..."
git push origin main
```

### 7. Client Questionnaire Cross-Reference

After implementing, verify completeness by extracting every distinct phrase from the client's response and searching the full site content:

```python
with open("content/en.json") as f:
    d = json.load(f)
content_text = json.dumps(d).lower()

phrases = [
    "streamlined collaboration between your home country",
    "married to a dutch national",
    # ... every standalone phrase from the response
]
for p in phrases:
    if p not in content_text:
        print(f"MISSING: {p}")
```

This catches case mismatches, phrasing drift, and omitted details.

### 8. Process Step Consistency Rule

When updating the process narrative, it lives in ALL of these places. Update every one:

- `home.process.steps`
- `processPage.process.steps`
- All landing pages: `landingInversor`, `landingTrust`, `landingLifestyle`, `landingEmpresa`

Any page that shows process steps must show the same 5 steps. Don't miss the landing pages — they're often 1-2 commits behind.

### 8. Verify New Fields Render

After adding a **new JSON field** that has no structural precedent in the file (e.g., `resultsParagraphs`, `resultsTitle`, `resultsEyebrow`), the data exists but the page component may not consume it.

**Process:**
1. Deploy the changes (build + service update)
2. Navigate to the page in a browser
3. Search the DOM for the expected text
4. If the text doesn't appear, the React component doesn't render that field

**Why this happens:** The Next.js App Router renders pages server-side. The page component reads specific keys from the content JSON (like `aboutPage.story.paragraphs`) and renders them. A new key like `aboutPage.story.resultsParagraphs` is only in the JSON — the component must be extended to read it.

**Fix options (in order of preference):**
- **Restructure the data** to use an existing key that IS rendered (e.g., add more items to `story.paragraphs` array instead of creating a new `resultsParagraphs`)
- **Extend the component** to read the new field (requires editing JSX)
- **Accept the gap** — data is version-controlled and deployable, component update is a separate task

**Detection before building:**
```bash
grep -r \"resultsParagraphs\" src/components/  # No match? Component doesn't render it
```

After implementing all content from a client's initial responses, produce a structured review document (`docs/client-update-questionnaire.md`) with these sections:

1. **Services** — confirm each service description
2. **Founder Story** — confirm paragraphs
3. **Process** — confirm step order and wording
4. **Pricing** — confirm package tiers
5. **Stats** — unverified numbers need confirmation
6. **Contact & Social** — WhatsApp, Facebook, address
7. **Integrations** — GA4, HubSpot, Mailchimp real IDs
8. **Photos** — AI placeholders need replacement
9. **Locales** — professional translation needed for specific languages
10. **Anything else** — open feedback

## Pitfalls

- **Patch can corrupt nested JSON** — If old_string matches in multiple locations or partial matches, patch may orphan sibling content. Always validate JSON after every patch operation. If complex, use Python JSON manipulation instead.
- **Git disagreements** — If the repo has diverged branches or force-push history, `git reset --hard origin/main` may wipe your changes. Always `git pull --rebase` first, or `git stash` before resetting.
- **EN vs ES key structures differ** — Never assume keys match. `content_es` might have `about` while `content_en` has `sobre`. Check both before syncing.
- **Drive folders with UUID filenames** — When a client shares a Drive folder with 40 files all named like UUIDs, download first, then classify by metadata (dimensions, format, naming). Don't assume you can use vision API — it may be down. Dimension-based classification + aspect ratio heuristics work well as fallback.
- **Full-height phone screenshots (1080×2400)** — These are almost always scroll-capture from a phone (KDP dashboard, Instagram profile). They WILL NOT fit in standard website sections. Either crop to the relevant portion or link to them as expandable proof elements rather than inline images.
- **.jfif files don't render in browsers** — Always convert to .png using PIL before committing.
- **Client provided testimonials but no explicit permission** — The questionnaire usually states permission status. Trust data from filled questionnaires. If unsure, use "Verified Client" instead of fabricated names.
- **Structural content duplication** — Content may be unique at the JSON key level (different content paths) but structurally identical at the data level (same item titles/groups/descriptions). See `references/structural-duplication-detection.md`.

## Related Skills

- `nexa-paraguay-deployment` — Section-to-page content ownership map, multi-locale deployment

", "path": "client-content-audit/SKILL.md", "skill_dir": "/root/.hermes/skills/client-content-audit", "linked_files": {"references": ["references/dayah-corrections-[REDACTED_PHONE].md", "references/structural-duplication-detection.md", "references/nexa-paraguay-content-audit-[REDACTED_PHONE].md", "references/nexa-paraguay-client-content-[REDACTED_PHONE].md", "references/two-audience-gate-pattern.md", "references/questionnaire-ABCDE-pattern.md", "references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md", "references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md"]}, "usage_hint": "To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'", "required_environment_variables": [], "required_commands": [], "missing_required_environment_variables": [], "missing_credential_files": [], "missing_required_commands": [], "setup_needed": false, "setup_skipped": false, "readiness_status": "available"}

This skill is already very rich and covers the content audit pattern. What's missing is the specific pattern of **template placeholder leakage** — when a client site inherits `content/en.json` or `content/gn.json` from a template and the secondary locales still have old placeholder data (like "Dayah LitWorks"). This is a different class of problem from "client provided content and I need to implement it" — it's a **post-scaffold content audit** that checks for residual template artifacts.

Let me also check the `ecommerce-codebase-audit` skill since Viajero is an e-commerce site and there may be learnings to add.

{"success": true, "name": "ecommerce-codebase-audit", "description": "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, auth cookie debugging, deployment follow-through, and post-deployment client readiness review.", "tags": ["nextjs", "ecommerce", "admin", "audit", "tailwind", "layout", "sidebar", "supabase", "auth", "cloudflare", "org-restructuring", "package-extraction"], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: ecommerce-codebase-audit description: "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, auth cookie debugging, deployment follow-through, and post-deployment client readiness review." version: 1.5.0 tags: [nextjs, ecommerce, admin, audit, tailwind, layout, sidebar, supabase, auth, cloudflare, org-restructuring, package-extraction] --- # Ecommerce Codebase Audit Inspect and improve a Next.js e-commerce admin panel. Focus on layout consistency, theme application, page structure, image assets, and auth flow. ## Audit Checklist ### Layout Consistency - [ ] Every admin page wraps content in `AdminShell` from `@/components/admin/admin-layout` - [ ] `AdminShell` includes the sidebar navigation (responsive: hidden on mobile, fixed on desktop) - [ ] Sidebar uses the same links and active-state highlighting across all pages - [ ] All pages render inside `` not just individually ### Theme System The admin theme uses emerald/zinc on a dark background (#0a0a0b / #0f0f10): ```tsx // Tailwind classes to look for bg-[#0f0f10] // sidebar + card backgrounds bg-zinc-800/60 // borders text-emerald-400 // active text bg-emerald-500/10 // active background text-zinc-400 // inactive text hover:bg-zinc-800/40 // hover state ``` - [ ] All pages use the same color tokens (not hardcoded different colors) - [ ] Active page shows `bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-400` with indicator dot - [ ] Inactive pages use `text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-800/40` ### Page Structure Each admin page follows this pattern: ```tsx 'use client' import { AdminShell, useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" export default function PageName() { const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null return ( <>

Page Title

{/* content */} ) } ``` - [ ] Pages use `{ authed } = useAdminAuth()` for auth guard - [ ] Pages use `AdminShell` wrapper (already in the root layout) - [ ] Pages use `fetch("/api/admin/*")` for data (not direct Supabase client) - [ ] Data fetched via `/api/admin/` routes that use service_role key server-side ### AdminShell Component Located in `@/components/admin/admin-layout` (or wherever it's imported from). The AdminShell provides: - Sidebar navigation with 4 groups: Operaciones, Catálogo, Marketing, Configuración - Mobile responsiveness (hidden sidebar on mobile, toggle with hamburger) - Auth guard via `useAdminAuth` - Consistent background and layout padding ## Auth Flow & Cookie Debugging ### The Core Trap: Two Auth Paths Crossing In Next.js + Supabase e-commerce projects, there are usually TWO auth paths that can conflict: **Path A — Consumer auth (useAuth):** - Login page calls `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` directly from the browser client - Supabase's `@supabase/ssr` browser client sets the session cookie automatically - `/mi-cuenta` reads session via `supabase.auth.getSession()` — works because cookie exists **Path B — Admin auth (useAdminAuth / /api/auth):** - Login page calls `/api/auth` with `action: "login"` - API route creates an SSR client with `createServerClient()`, but often has `setAll() {}` empty - **The session cookie never gets set on the response** — the API returns tokens, but no cookie - Admin layout falls back to localStorage or custom cookie **Common failure modes:** 1. **Login works (gets tokens) but /mi-cuenta shows unauthenticated** — consumer login page fetches `/api/auth` instead of calling Supabase directly. Fix: use `useAuth().login()` (Supabase browser client) which sets the correct session cookie. 2. **Login works but /admin redirects back to /login** — admin layout's `useAdminAuth()` calls `/api/auth?action=me` with a token from localStorage, but the API route's token verification path fails. Fix: ensure the API route can verify tokens against Supabase REST API (not just SSR cookies). ### Fix Checklist - [ ] Consumer login uses Supabase browser client directly (`supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`), NOT a custom `/api/auth` endpoint - [ ] After successful login, store session tokens in `localStorage` for admin layout fallback: `localStorage.setItem("elviajero_admin_session", JSON.stringify({ access_token, refresh_token }))` - [ ] Admin layout checks localStorage first, then Supabase SSR cookie, then custom cookie — triple fallback - [ ] API route `action: "me"` verifies tokens via `fetch(${supabaseUrl}/auth/v1/user)` not `supabase.auth.getUser()` - [ ] Service-role client for profile queries uses `createClient(url, serviceRoleKey)` — passing service key as second arg, not in headers - [ ] Role check queries DB profiles table (not JWT claim) — `profiles.role` not `user.role` ### Debugging Auth Issues ```bash # Check if API route is reachable curl -sX POST https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/api/auth \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"action":"login","email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","password":"test"}' | head -c 200 # Check if session cookie is set curl -sv https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/mi-cuenta 2>&1 | grep -i 'set-cookie' # Check server logs docker service logs elviajero-comercio_web --tail 30 --no-task-ids ``` ## Visual Asset Workflow ### Full Asset Overhaul Pattern When doing a complete visual assets redesign (inventory, generation, deployment): #### Phase 1: Inventory ```bash # List all files on disk find public/images -type f | sort # List all code references grep -rohP '"/images/[^"'"'"']+' --include='*.{tsx,ts,json,css}' | sed 's/^"//' | sort -u # Cross-reference: 3 outcomes per image # 1. Referenced + on disk = OK # 2. Referenced + missing = FIX (add file) # 3. On disk + no reference = ORPHAN (delete) # Size audit — small = programmatic (low quality) find public/images -name "*.png" -size -50k ``` #### Phase 2: Categorization | Type | Size threshold | Keep as | Upgrade? | |------|---------------|---------|----------| | SVGs | varies | Vector | Only if they're emoji text | | Real photos | >50KB | PNG | No | | Programmatic renders | <50KB | PNG | Yes — regenerate with richer Pillow composites | | Icons | 4-8KB | PNG | Intentionally small — minimalist silhouettes | | Hero backgrounds | <20KB | PNG | Yes — add atmosphere, silhouettes, gradients | #### Phase 3: Generation (no AI API available) Use Pillow for progressive enhancement rounds: **Round 1 — Placeholders:** ```python # Sky gradient + bokeh circles + category silhouettes # Write to a generate script, deploy, iterate ``` **Round 2 — Richer details:** - Tree silhouettes, stars, glowing tent, moon - River reflections, fishing rod, sun at horizon - Mountain trails, hiker with backpack - Headlight beams, road lines, car approaching - Dust clouds, motorcycle, fence - Fields, barn, sun, clouds **Round 3 — AI (if REPLICATE_API_TOKEN or equivalent available):** ```python if call_replicate(prompt, w, h, path): print("AI generated") else: render_fallback(path) # keep best Pillow version ``` **Round 4 — Self-critique:** After deploying, review each asset and write a brutal critique doc. Then regenerate the worst offenders in the next round. #### Phase 4: Deployment Pitfalls **Cloudflare cache (IMPORTANT):** - CF sets `cache-control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` — 1 YEAR cache - No CF API key? Only option is **rename the file** (logo.png → logo-v2.png) - Then update ALL code references to point to new filename - Verify with curl, not browser: `curl -sk URL/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B"` - Then tell the user to hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) **Docker layer cache:** - If files exist locally but return 404 on deploy, the Docker COPY was cached - Fix: `docker build --no-cache` or touch a file in the COPY layer **Verify the right file is served:** ```bash # Check HTML references curl -sk https://example.com/ | grep -oP '(logo|favicon)-v2' # Check file directly curl -sk https://example.com/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B" ``` ### Key Project-Specific Patterns (El Viajero) - Product images: `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/products/*.png` (34 products) - Category backgrounds (dynamic SVG): `/images/categories/${slug}.svg` — slug values in `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` - Category heroes: heroImage prop → `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` → `/images/marketing/hero-*.png` - Hero carousel: content-driven array in `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/hero-carousel-*.png` - Testimonials: `"image"` field in `content/es.json` → `/images/testimonials/avatar-*.png` - Logo: `header.tsx` → `/images/logo.png` (or `logo-v2.png` after cache-bust) - Favicon: `layout.tsx` → `/images/favicon.png` (or `favicon-v2.png`) - OG image: `layout.tsx` → `/images/og-viajero.svg` - PWA icons: `manifest.json` → `/images/icon-192.png`, `/images/icon-512.png` ### Generation Recipes **Category icons (128x128):** Create a Python script with `ImageDraw` — tent, fish, umbrella, backpack, car, motorcycle, barn silhouettes on green circle background. These are intentionally small (4-8KB). **Hero banners (1920x600):** Create scene composites: 1. Sky gradient (top to bottom, warm/cool depending on scene) 2. Ground layer (dark rectangle) 3. Scene elements (tree silhouettes, stars, moon, tent, river, fishing rod, mountains, road, headlights, barn, fence, sun) 4. Bokeh overlay (random circles with low alpha) 5. Gaussian blur (radius 0.3-0.5) for polish **Testimonial avatars (200x200):** If no AI available, create colored circles with basic face features (eyes, smile, hair shape, shirt). Tell the client these are placeholders for real customer photos. **PWA icons:** Render from the logo at 192x192 and 512x512 with anti-aliasing. ## Multi-Locale Content Updates (PAB Sites) When updating content across all locales (en/es/nl/de) in a PAB (Paragu-AI Builder) project: ### Safe approach vs. pitfalls 1. **Python JSON manipulation is safer than `patch` tool for large content changes.** The `patch` tool works well for small string replacements, but when replacing JSON blocks spanning 50+ lines, it can match the wrong text, leave orphaned content, and produce valid-but-wrong JSON. This happened when replacing the `processPage` section — patch matched a subset, leaving trailing orphan that was syntactically valid but structurally wrong. 2. **When `patch` goes wrong: stop, don't stack.** If the first patch produces unexpected results (orphan content, duplicated sections, lost keys), do NOT apply more patches on top. Switch immediately to Python `json.load()/json.dump()` — read the broken file, fix the data structure in memory, and write it back clean. Stacking patches compounds the mistakes and makes the file increasingly fragile to further edits. The `processPage` fix required three patches to clean up what one wrong match did. 3. **`patch` can match content from a previously-applied patch** — If a file was already modified, the old_string might not match what's actually in the file. Always read the file fresh before patching, and verify with a direct key inspection after (`python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('f')); print(d.get('targetKey',{}).keys())"`). 4. **Shell variable expansion fails in `execute_code`'s multi-line Python strings** — `for f in $FILES; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))"; done` breaks because `$f` isn't expanded inside the Python string. Use explicit file paths, a temp script, or Python's own `glob` module instead. 5. **Always validate JSON structure after content changes, not just syntax.** A file can be valid JSON but structurally wrong (missing keys, wrong step counts, orphan content). After validation: ```bash python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('file'))" # syntax check ``` Also verify structural integrity: ```python d["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] # should have 5 steps d["home"]["services"]["groups"] # should have 3 groups d["aboutPage"]["story"]["paragraphs"] # should have 3 paragraphs ``` 6. **When deleting orphaned content from a botched patch, read the file with JSON parser first**, then find the structural key that contains the orphan data. Delete it from the Python dict and re-write. Don't try to surgically remove lines with patch. 7. **Process step consistency rule:** The same process narrative lives in 6 places across the site. Update ALL of these together in the same script block — missing any one creates inconsistency: - `home.process.steps` (homepage) - `processPage.process.steps` (process page) - `landingInversor.process.steps`, `landingTrust.process.steps`, `landingLifestyle.process.steps`, `landingEmpresa.process.steps` (LinkedIn ad landing pages) 8. **Process for adding new content sections across locales:** - Step 1: Define the content data structure in a Python dict with all localizations - Step 2: Load each locale file, apply transformations, write back - Step 3: Validate JSON syntax for all files - Step 4: Verify structural keys match expectations (count groups, steps, paragraphs) ```python for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]: with open(f"content/{lang}.json") as f: d = json.load(f) d["parentSection"]["newKey"] = translations[lang] with open(f"content/{lang}.json", "w") as f: json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) ``` 9. **Section structure for the 'Our Services' pattern:** ```python data["home"]["services"] = { "eyebrow": "Our Services", "title": "Section title", "groups": [ {"id": "group-id", "title": "Group Name", "subtitle": "Group subtitle.", "items": [{"title": "Service", "description": "Desc.", "icon": "IconName"}]} ] } ``` 10. **Section structure for the 'Story' pattern:** ```python data["aboutPage"]["story"] = { "title": "Our Story", "eyebrow": "Tagline", "paragraphs": ["Para 1.", "Para 2.", "Para 3."] } ``` 11. **Section structure for the 'Process steps' pattern:** ```python data["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] = [ {"number": 1, "title": "Step Name", "description": "Long description.", "icon": "IconName", "image": {"$img": "process.key"}, "duration": "Week 1"} ] ``` 12. **Landing page Calendly URL pattern:** ```python base = "https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/consulta" utm = f"utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign={campaign}" url = f"{base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-{placement}-{lang}" ``` ## Deployment ### Docker Deployment After modifying content assets (images, content JSON, or static files): 1. **Rebuild** — `npm run build` catches missing images and type errors 2. **Rebuild Docker image** — `docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .` 3. **Deploy** — `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false` 4. **Verify** — `curl -sI https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/ | head -5` 5. **Force rolling update** — if same image tag, `docker service update --force elviajero-comercio_web` Never assume changes to static content or images are live — Docker rebuild and stack deploy are required. Browser cache is NOT the server — always curl first. ### Production Monitoring After every deploy, add: - Healthcheck endpoint at `/api/health` returning `{status, timestamp, uptime}` - Docker HEALTHCHECK in Dockerfile (every 30s, 40s start grace, 3 retries) - Cron healthcheck via Hermes: every 15m, auto-redeploy on failure - Shell check script: `/root/scripts/check-{project}.sh` ### Docker Build Timeout `docker build` with npm install can take >120s on cold cache. Set terminal timeout to 300s for builds. If it times out, retry — the npm cache is warm on the second run and it will complete faster. #### Next.js `pages/` Folder Conflict (v16+) Next.js 16+ errors with "`pages` and `app` directories should be under the same folder" when a `pages/` data directory exists at project root alongside `src/app/`. Fix: 1. Rename `pages/` to `nexa-pages/` 2. Update all `page.tsx` references from `'pages'` to `'nexa-pages'` 3. Update Dockerfile to copy `nexa-pages/` into the standalone output 4. Run `docker build --no-cache` after rename (Docker layer cache may use old path) #### Docker Standalone + Data Files Next.js `output: "standalone"` produces a `server.js` that does `process.chdir(__dirname)` — so `process.cwd()` points to `.next/standalone/`, not the app root. Data files read at runtime via `readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), ...))` must be copied into `.next/standalone/`: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./ COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./.next/standalone/nexa-pages COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./.next/standalone/content ``` Without this, the container runs fine but returns static HTML with fallback data because `readFileSync` silently fails and the catch block returns defaults. #### The "Welcome" Bug (Static SSG Fallback) When a PAB page always renders "Welcome" regardless of content changes, the root cause is in the page component's fallback chain: ```tsx // BROKEN: home.json has 'titleKey' not 'title' — always falls through {homePage.title || content?.title || 'Welcome'} // FIXED: use siteName from the locale JSON {content?.siteName || 'Nexa Paraguay'} ``` Check that `getPages()` and `getContent()` actually read files via `readFileSync`/`readdirSync` at build time. The `catch` block silently swallowing the error is the symptom — verify the files are accessible during the Docker build stage. #### `window.gtag` Type Conflicts When a project uses `declare global { interface Window { gtag?: any } }` and imports from `@ai-whisperers/seo/analytics` (which also declares it), you get: ``` Type error: All declarations of 'gtag' must have identical modifiers. ``` **Fix:** Use `(window as any).gtag` everywhere instead of declaring `interface Window`: ```typescript const w = window as any w.gtag("event", "page_view", data) ``` Apply to all GA4 tracking files: `components/analytics.tsx`, `lib/analytics.ts`. ## Traefik + Docker Swarm Routing Setup When deploying a new Next.js client site to an existing Docker Swarm + Traefik infrastructure (paragu-ai.com VPS at 72.61.44.159): ### 1. DNS Record (Cloudflare) Add an A record in the Cloudflare dashboard for the paragu-ai.com zone: ``` Type: A Name: {site-name} Content: 72.61.44.159 Proxy status: Proxied (orange cloud) ``` ### 2. docker-compose.yml (Traefik Labels) The template's `docker-compose.yml` has superspuma's labels. Update them: ```yaml services: web: build: . image: {site-name}:prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 resources: limits: { memory: 512M, cpus: '0.5' } reservations: { memory: 128M, cpus: '0.1' } labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.rule=Host(`{site-name}.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services.{site-name}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" networks: agent-net: external: true ``` Pitfall: the `image` field in docker-compose.yml must match the `docker build -t` tag. ### 3. Deploy (Manual on VPS) ```bash ssh root@72.61.44.159 cd /root/{repo-name} git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) # for @ai-whisperers npm packages npm ci --legacy-peer-deps npm run build docker build -t {site-name}:prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name} ``` After deploy, verify: ```bash curl -sI https://{site-name}.paragu-ai.com/ | grep "HTTP/" ``` ### 4. Docker Build GITHUB_TOKEN Issue The standard Docker multi-stage build (template's Dockerfile) runs `npm install` inside the build container but doesn't have access to the GITHUB_TOKEN for @ai-whisperers GitHub Packages registry. **Two solutions:** **Option A (simple):** Build on the host, then use a production-only Dockerfile that copies the already-built output: ```dockerfile # Dockerfile.prod — used after `npm run build` on host FROM node:20-alpine AS runner WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0" RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs COPY --chown=nextjs:nodejs . . USER nextjs EXPOSE 3000 CMD ["node_modules/.bin/next", "start"] ``` ```bash # On VPS: npm run build # host build has GITHUB_TOKEN docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t site:prod . # just ships node_modules + .next docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml site ``` **Option B (Docker build-arg):** Pass the token as a build arg and set it in the .npmrc at build time: ```dockerfile FROM node:20-alpine AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY package.json package-lock.json* .npmrc ./ ARG NODE_AUTH_TOKEN RUN echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc && \ npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` ```bash docker build --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) -t site:prod . ``` **Pitfall:** Docker BuildKit blocks ARG values from ENV for security ("Do not use ARG or ENV instructions for sensitive data"). Option A is more reliable. ### 5. CI/CD Workflow The reusable ci-cd workflows (Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd) may not be accessible from new repos due to permissions. Example standalone deploy.yml as fallback: ```yaml name: Deploy on: push: branches: [main] jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20' cache: 'npm' - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps - run: npm run build - name: Setup SSH run: | mkdir -p ~/.ssh echo "${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ssh-keyscan -H ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true - name: Deploy via SSH run: | ssh ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}@${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} \ "cd /root/{repo-name} && git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main && \ export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) && npm ci --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build && \ docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t {site-name}:prod . && \ docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name}" ``` ## Google Maps Browser Navigation for Search-Engine-Blocked Research When search engines (Google, DuckDuckGo, Bing, Brave) block automated queries with CAPTCHAs/IP blocks, use browser_navigate to Google Maps as a workaround: ### Pattern 1. Navigate to Google Maps search URL directly: ```bash https://www.google.com/maps/search/{query}+{location} ``` 2. The browser snapshot shows results as readable `article` elements with: - Company name (clickable) - Rating (stars) - Address - Phone number - Hours - Website link (when available) 3. Click a result to view more details in the side panel. 4. To refine, clear the combobox and type a new query, then press Enter. ### Queries That Work Spanish-language queries work better for Paraguayan businesses: ``` "constructora civil Asuncion Paraguay" "vidrio templado laminado fachada Asuncion" "fachada vidrio aluminio Asuncion Paraguay" "desarrolladora inmobiliaria Asuncion Paraguay" "arquitecto Asuncion Paraguay fachada" ``` ### Limitations - Only shows ~7 results per view (Google Maps limited view without login) - No API access to extract full data programmatically - Phone numbers and emails must be copied manually from snapshots - Results are biased toward Google's ranking, not comprehensive - Works for Paraguay because Google Maps has good coverage there ## B2B Lead-Gen Conversion (e-commerce template → B2B site) When converting the e-commerce template (template-nextjs-client) into a B2B lead-generation site (construction materials, industrial supply, wholesale), follow this pattern: ### What to REMOVE from the template ``` B2C pages to delete: app/tienda/ → product store with cart/checkout app/envio/ → shipping info app/promociones/ → promotions app/producto/[slug]/ → individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) B2C components to delete: search-autocomplete.tsx → no product search for B2B dark-mode-toggle.tsx → B2B stays light theme payment-methods.tsx → no payments on lead-gen site quick-order.tsx → no quick ordering product-card.tsx → replace with spec-card pattern hero-carousel.tsx → use static hero for B2B promo-carousel.tsx → no promos bottom-nav.tsx → not needed for B2B mobile empty-state.tsx → no shopping experience recently-viewed.tsx → no browsing history wishlist.ts / lib/ → no wishlists ``` ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Full list of Fun4Me artifacts to hunt when converting template When converting the template to a B2B site, these Fun4Me-specific traces survive even after page/component deletion: | File | What to fix | |------|-------------| | `app/contacto/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"`, Instagram `@fun4me_py`, "Estado del pedido" / "Reclamo / devolución" quick links | | `app/nosotros/page.tsx` | `brand.story` fallback to "bienestar íntimo con total privacidad" (sex shop), "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, purple heart emoji, `/tienda` link | | `app/sitemap.ts` | Hardcoded `base = "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com"`, fun4me page paths | | `content/es.json` | `analytics.ga4` empty string (needs real GA4 ID), `home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref: "/tienda"` | | `layout.tsx` | Theme-color `#1B5E20` (green, not blue B2B), metadata title fallbacks | | `components/` | Any file still importing `@/components/search-autocomplete` or `dark-mode-toggle` | | `docker-compose.yml` | Traefik labels referencing old site name (`superspuma`) | | Blog post `[slug]/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` | After content/es.json fixes, check the about/nosotros pages still reference `brand` keys (which were Fun4Me-specific) instead of `nosotros` keys (which have the B2B content). Fix by rewriting page.tsx to use the correct content section. | Page | Purpose | Content Pattern | |------|---------|-----------------| | /productos | Product catalog with specs | Technical data + "request quote" CTA (no prices) | | /proyectos | Portfolio gallery | Image + building name + scope + year | | /servicios | Service listing | 5-6 service cards with icons | | /cotizar | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product type (multi-select), qty, timeline → WhatsApp | ### Content Model (es.json) Home page sections for B2B: 1. **Hero**: gradient background, headline focusing on B2B value prop, two CTAs (quote form + WhatsApp) 2. **Features grid**: 4-column cards (stock local, certified quality, tech support, payment terms) 3. **Product preview**: 4 cards linking to /productos anchors 4. **Testimonials**: 2-3 B2B client quotes 5. **Final CTA**: Dark gradient, WhatsApp button Product catalog structure: ```json { "products": [ { "id": "product-slug", "name": "Product Name", "description": "Technical description for B2B buyers", "features": ["Feature 1", "Feature 2"], "applications": ["Building type 1", "Building type 2"], "cta": "Request quote for X" } ] } ``` Quote form on /cotizar: collects name, company, email, phone, project type (dropdown), product type (multi-select buttons), approximate qty/m2, timeline, message. On submit → opens WhatsApp with pre-formatted message. ### Theme Use a LIGHT theme for construction/B2B (not dark): ```json { "primary": "#0F62FE", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA" } ``` Remove dark-mode-toggle. The template already supports light mode via content/tokens.json `"theme": "light"`. ### Header/Footer Adaptation Header: replace Fun4Me brand with client name. Replace nav items. Footer: replace brand name, update columns for B2B context. ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Debugging When the template has `@ai-whisperers/*` dependencies with `^0.1.0` ranges that don't match the published registry: 1. Check actual latest versions: ```bash for pkg in @ai-whisperers/ui @ai-whisperers/admin @ai-whisperers/seo; do echo -n "$pkg: "; npm view $pkg version 2>&1 done ``` 2. Published versions differ per package: | Package | Latest | |---------|--------| | @ai-whisperers/ui | 0.4.0 | | @ai-whisperers/admin | 0.2.1 | | @ai-whisperers/seo | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/theme | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/auth | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/commerce | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/whatsapp | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/i18n | 0.2.0 | 3. Set each to its correct version (NOT all to same). The `^0.1.0` range in the template was never updated after the packages reached 0.2.x/0.4.x. 4. Some packages (like `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest`) don't exist — remove from devDependencies. 5. @ai-whisperers/seo 0.2.0 has an empty dist directory — the AnalyticsProvider import fails. Workaround: remove the AnalyticsProvider from layout.tsx and add GA4 via plain `
